answer: The verse appears in the second stanza of chapter 168 ('Rahiye ab aisi jagah chal kar jahan koi na ho') of Diwan-E-Ghalib (work slug: mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c). In the English translation it reads: 'A house I'll build of neither wall nor stone, / No neighbor there, no guard, and be alone.' This corresponds to the Urdu line beginning 'Be-dar-o-deewar sa ek ghar banaya' — a house without door or wall, with no neighbour (hamsaya), no companion, and no door-keeper/guard.

tool_calls.result_summary: Three-stanza translation; second stanza: 'A house I'll build of neither wall nor stone, / No neighbor there, no guard, and be alone.'
